probe_round.head: 55f304a
probe_round.duration_note: Real per-probe cost is a full controller run (~13-15 min: worktrees lack the untracked out/ substrate, and QA-013 source-manifest binding makes bare dotnet test detect any source mutation as substrate drift, so honest mutant verification requires the full run-spike.sh pipeline). 5 probes dispatched in parallel worktrees to fit the 30-minute wall-clock budget; survival criterion is controller exit 0 with an all-pass suite.

probes.mutation: full sha256 equality weakened to 8-hex-prefix comparison on the decoys/-dir sanctioning branch
probes.detail: Controller failed for a mutation-independent environmental defect: FindRepoRoot() (SeamCommon.cs:368) checks Directory.Exists(.git) but linked worktrees have a .git FILE, so the walk ascends to the outer repo; P12 closure paths come out worktree-prefixed, both routes fail P12, 6 suite tests fail. Counterfactual: sibling M2 worktree (HarnessCore unmutated) shows the identical P12 failure.
probes.mutation_specific_signal: SURVIVOR-BY-SIGNAL: startup gate passed under the prefix predicate and both armor tests (QA015 wrong-digest decoy, QA022 tag filter) passed — no test discriminates full-digest vs prefix comparison. Killing test would assert the comparison consumes full 64-hex digests (unit level).
probes.round_level_finding: PR-001: spike suite cannot produce a valid verdict from any linked git worktree (gitfile-unaware FindRepoRoot, SeamCommon.cs:368) — poisons all worktree-isolated probe verification; also affects any developer using git worktrees.

probes.verdict: 7/7 KILLED — zero survivors, zero hangs, zero untyped crashes; every refusal typed
cases: C1 schema whitespace -> exit 20 startup digest refusal (INV-009)
C2 mask over-masking attempt -> killed by digest anchor before mask semantics read
C3 retired v1 registry row digest edit -> caught ONLY by suite tests (canonical); variance runtime ACCEPTED it (depth finding PR-002)
C4 duplicate v2 registry row -> exit 20 startup append-only refusal
C5 probe-manifest P09/A deleted -> suite + aggregation typed INV-006 refusal (late-phase catch, no startup anchor)
C6 expected-loaded route-a entry removed -> P03 fail route A only (INV-002)
C7 option-manifest canary version edit -> P11 fail (INV-007/OQ-003)
depth_findings: PR-002: registry append-only HISTORY is suite-enforced only — ValidateSchemaFile (Components.cs:246) checks duplicate rows + current-version digest only; a falsified retired row passes route-child startup gates and aggregator; variance/nested runs (which never run the suite) emit reports under falsified schema lineage. Blast radius: history falsification only — a wrong CURRENT schema still cannot slip runtime.
PR-003: probe-manifest has no route-child startup anchor — tampered manifest runs the full probe set and is refused at aggregation (typed, fail-closed, but late-phase).
Over-masking is transitively unreachable: byte-digest anchor kills any mask tampering before semantics are evaluated.
Confirms PR-001: pristine-tree worktree runs fail P12 A+B + 6 suite tests; exit 0 unreachable from linked worktrees — baseline-fingerprint diffing mandatory for all worktree probes.
probes.process_note: F2 accidentally deleted a sibling probe's in-flight scratchpad logs + shared runner.sh before namespacing its own state under scratchpad/f2/; a foreign canonical run also executed inside F2's worktree before its cases (guarded: every case preceded by git-status + foreign-process checks).

probes.mutation: anyProbeFailed predicate narrowed to exclude shared probes: status=="fail" -> status=="fail" && route!="shared". A shared P02 failure (build-receipt nonzero exit or SDK-pin mismatch) falls through to exit 20 INCOMPLETE via anyProbeNotPass instead of the typed exit 10 ProbeFailure. Reachable and non-equivalent: P02 is the only shared probe that can be status=fail (P04 failures record as Incomplete, P01 is partitioned per-route).
probes.detail: Baseline-differential per PR-001 methodology: mutant and pristine full-pipeline runs produced byte-identical failure sets (both 212/218; the 6 failures are the known environmental worktree artifacts; identical summary 'suite: failure; route children A=10 B=10; aggregator exit 10; controller exit 1'). Every exit-mapping-pinning test (QA016_MissingRouteReport/ForgedRunId/Malformed exit 20, QA016_AllPassVariance exit 0, QA022 mixed/unadjudicated) passed against the mutated binary, and the mutated line WAS exercised. Fail-closed nonzero is preserved, but the CI-facing typed exit contract is broken for the shared-probe failure class: retry-on-INCOMPLETE CI logic would retry a build-receipt/SDK-pin violation instead of hard-failing it.
probes.why_missed: Every test that launches the aggregator binary writes a passing build receipt (QaFixRound2Tests.WriteReceipts exit:0 + SpecConstants.SdkPin; same in QaFixRound1Tests); QA005 discards the exit code; Inv006VerdictTests.SharedProbeFailure_DeniesBothRoutes exercises ComputeRouteVerdict in-process with no exit-code assertion. No test constructs a failing P02 end-to-end and asserts the aggregator exit code.
probes.killing_test_candidate: Launch the aggregator with a valid nonce-bound build receipt carrying nonzero exit (or mismatched sdk_version) and assert ExitCodes.ProbeFailure (10), not merely nonzero.

probes.mutation: anyFailed predicate enum-comparison swap: p.Status != ProbeStatus.Pass -> p.Status == ProbeStatus.Fail, collapsing the tri-state probe status to binary inside the matrix. A child exiting 0 while its report admits an Incomplete probe is no longer flagged ExitReportMismatch (fail-closed cell weakened); exit 10 with only-incomplete probes gets the wrong diagnostic. Honest-harness paths unchanged (harness maps any Incomplete to exit 20, bypassing anyFailed), so not trivially fatal.
probes.detail: Pristine-baseline failure-set diff (PR-001 methodology): mechanical set diff of failing test names vs recorded baseline is EMPTY — both runs fail exactly the 6 known environmental worktree tests (Inv006 ForgedExtraReportOnDisk, Inv009 CommittedSample, Inv010 RunTwice, Inv012 P12 A+B, Inv014 StaleRepoLocalArtifacts), 212/218 passing including every MapExit/verdict-matrix test (Inv006, Inv013, QA-019, QA-022, QA-024). Zero failures attributable to the mutation.
probes.why_missed: The only test constructing a ProbeStatus.Incomplete probe (ProhibitionTests.cs:26) goes through ComputeRouteVerdict, never MapExit; no test covers the matrix cells 'exit 0 + incomplete probe in report' or 'exit 10 + only-incomplete probes'. The existing mismatch test (Inv006VerdictTests.cs:250-259) covers only the exit-10-with-all-pass cell. Adjacent to deferred DF-003 (QA-025: exit 20 + all-pass report cell uncovered) — the MapExit matrix has a cluster of untested cells; killing tests and the DF-003 fix could land together.
probes.killing_test_candidate: Unit-level MapExit matrix tests: (a) child exit 0 + report containing an Incomplete probe -> assert ExitReportMismatch; (b) child exit 10 + only-incomplete probes -> assert correct attribution (not the all-pass-mismatch diagnostic).

probes.target: run-spike.sh CONFIG input surfaces: run-config.json, env-profiles.json, z3-pin.json, net8-control-pin.json (canonical no-arg invocation)
probes.verdict: 7/7 failed closed on the canonical operator surface - no exit-0 survivors, no crashes; 4 fast typed refusals, 1 slow fail-closed (C2), 2 slow suite-gate-only kills (C6/C7)
cases: C1 run-config wall_clock_bound_seconds=0 -> exit 20 typed WallClockExpiry refusal in 8s (QA-007/RS-015 supervisor SIGTERM)

C2 run-config truncated to invalid JSON -> NO fast refusal: config_int fell open to the 1800s default, full pipeline ran, fail-closed only via end-of-run suite ~9-10 min later, controller exit 1 (depth finding PR-004)
C3 duplicate wall_clock_bound_seconds (first 1, JSON-last 1800) -> exit 20 typed refusal in 9s under the FIRST-occurrence value, contradicting JSON last-wins (depth finding PR-005)
C4 env-profiles restore launch-class key renamed -> exit 20 typed EA-008 missing-key refusal in 4s
C5 env-profiles emptied -> exit 20 typed EA-008 refusal at restore in 2s, but provisioning had already LAUNCHED under an empty env dict (depth finding PR-006)
C6 z3-pin.json sha256 flipped to valid-format wrong digest -> suite-gate kill only (exit 1, 279s): 6 baseline + Inv003 Z3Pin committed-consistency test (depth finding PR-007)
C7 net8-control-pin.json runtime_version digit swapped for fullwidth U+FF19 -> suite-gate kill only (exit 1, 275s): 6 baseline + Inv013 runtime-pin test (depth finding PR-007)
depth_findings: PR-004: controller performs zero structural JSON validation of committed run-config and config_int is fail-open on missing keys (falls back to the 1800s default). A corrupt committed config gets no fast startup refusal - detection is deferred entirely to the end-of-run suite (>9 min in this environment). Eventually fail-closed, but defeats the fast-fail contract for the malformed-config surface.

PR-005: config_int resolves duplicate JSON keys to the FIRST occurrence, contradicting JSON last-wins semantics. Benign in C3 (still a fast typed refusal) but a latent inconsistency: a hostile duplicate steers the effective bound to the attacker's first value.

PR-006: the provision launch class has no require_env_key guard - with an empty/missing env-profiles, provisioning launches under an empty env dict (env-audit records class=provision env={}); INV-003 decoy-first PATH construction is absent for the provision cell. The typed refusal lands one phase later at restore, before any build, so net outcome is still fail-closed.

PR-007: z3-pin.json and net8-control-pin.json are NOT consumed by the execution path (provision-z3.sh mirrors the Z3 digest as an internal literal; run-spike.sh:811-812 hardcodes the net8 URL+SHA). Pin-file corruption is caught only by canonical-run suite tests (Inv003/Inv013). Hypothesis, not demonstrated (worktree P12 blocks a clean check): a variance/nested run skips the suite phase, so on a clean checkout a corrupted pin file could ride to controller exit 0 undetected. Same suite-only enforcement shape as PR-002.
probes.baseline_note: Worktree environmental baseline (PR-001) re-confirmed by intersecting C6/C7 suite failures: P12 A+B + exactly the 6 known tests.
probes.process_note: All 7 planned cases ran; F1's private f1/ scratch namespace survived the F2 deletion accident. Config restored to pristine 55f304a, run roots cleaned, no stray processes.

dispositions.survivors_M1_M2_M3signal: generate killing tests now (one-shot test-gen agent; user approves tests before they are committed in the audit-mini phase)
dispositions.PR-002_PR-007: fix in mini-audit (runtime validation of retired registry rows; execution path consumes / startup-anchors the z3+net8 pin files)
dispositions.PR-003_PR-004_PR-005_PR-006: fix all in mini-audit (probe-manifest startup anchor, structural run-config validation at startup, duplicate-key refusal, provision-class env guard)

probe_round.summary: 5/5 probes reported. Survivors: M1 (MapExit tri-state collapse untested), M2 (aggregator typed-exit contract for shared-probe failure untested), M3 survivor-by-signal (digest-prefix weakening undetected; M3 run itself INVALID per PR-001). Killed: F2 7/7, F1 7/7 (fail-closed, 3 slow/late). Advisory findings PR-001..PR-007.
